title: locally build images doesn't show up in podman desktop neither in other terminal sessions
body:
### Issue Description

I've been having an issue with building the images with `podman build`, most likely its an SELinux labeling issue. I've also addressed it in a discussion [link](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17702#discussioncomment-12852699). For the time being to mitigate the issue I'm disabling selinux enforcing `sudo setenforce 0`, I'm using a bash script to build all the images. When I do `podman images` in the vs-code terminal where i build my images with the bash script I'm able to see the local image I built but when i run `podman images` in anyother terminal session I can only see the images I pulled from some remote repo. Also the local images aren't visible in the podman-desktop

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build local images with selinux enforcing disabled (`sudo setenforce 0`). Something like in the below bash script
```bash
#!/bin/bash

BLUE='\e[34m'
GREEN='\e[32m'
RED='\e[31m'
RESET='\e[0m'

timestamp=$(date +%y%m.%d.%H%M)

#set relative project root path
projectDir=`cd ../..;pwd`

declare -a arr=(
    # "user-ms"
    "template"
    )

count=1

sudo setenforce 0

for i in "${arr[@]}"
do
    echo -e "${BLUE}[$count/${#arr[@]}] Building $i$RESET"
    podman build --file "$projectDir/$i/Containerfile" --build-arg GITHUB_USERNAME=$GITHUB_USERNAME --build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN=$GITHUB_TOKEN --build-arg IS_PROD_ENV=$IS_PROD_ENV --tag "$i-local" "$projectDir/$i/"
    EXITCODE=$?
    if [ $EXITCODE != 0 ]; then
        echo -e "${RED}Build failed with exit code $EXITCODE$RESET"
        exit
    fi
    ((count++))
done
echo -e "${GREEN}[DONE] Building service images"

sudo setenforce 1
```

2. In a different terminal session run `podman images` and do the same in the same terminal session as the build task.
3. Check podman desktop for the images.


### Describe the results you received

When running `podman images` after building the image on the vs-code terminal I get 
```shell
harshit@fedora:~/www/kz/local-setup$ date
Saturday 19 April 2025 02:00:06 PM IST
harshit@fedora:~/www/kz/local-setup$ podman images
REPOSITORY                TAG          IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
localhost/template-local  latest       102fafff90ee  23 hours ago  34.7 MB
<none>                    <none>       49362a835edf  23 hours ago  819 MB
<none>                    <none>       9b7b40f92c65  39 hours ago  45.9 MB
<none>                    <none>       ec0cc87721fb  39 hours ago  832 MB
<none>                    <none>       a3531fb59c7e  39 hours ago  45.8 MB
<none>                    <none>       aba417924e9a  39 hours ago  832 MB
<none>                    <none>       58df7dd8892a  39 hours ago  45.9 MB
<none>                    <none>       d77fe7a01fd8  39 hours ago  838 MB
<none>                    <none>       39faca41e6a1  39 hours ago  273 MB
<none>                    <none>       f3a851899494  39 hours ago  273 MB
docker.io/library/golang  1.24-alpine  dce68b1cd229  2 weeks ago   273 MB
docker.io/library/alpine  3.21         aded1e1a5b37  2 months ago  8.13 MB
docker.io/library/nginx   latest       4cad75abc83d  2 months ago  196 MB
harshit@fedora:~/www/kz/local-setup$ 
```
I've also attached date and time for reference and verification.

now when i open a different terminal and run `podman images`, I see
```bash
harshit@fedora:~$ date
Saturday 19 April 2025 02:03:17 PM IST
harshit@fedora:~$ podman images
REPOSITORY               TAG               IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
localhost/podman-pause   5.4.2-1743552000  7fcdaa138d1b  23 hours ago   763 kB
docker.io/library/nginx  latest            4cad75abc83d  2 months ago   196 MB
quay.io/podman/hello     latest            5dd467fce50b  10 months ago  787 kB
harshit@fedora:~$
```
the pod infra container `podman-pause` and `hello` is also missing from the first output. I seems like they have totally different context.

Also the images of podman-desktop are below

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c307ccb-da5a-42c5-8fc0-7ee94303fda9)

### Describe the results you expected

It is expected to see all the images built and pulled as we do `podman images` anywhere and in any terminal session also on the podman desktop

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I'm on fedora 41

### Additional information

If I do `podman run` on the same terminal session of vs-code then i get successful run.
```bash
harshit@fedora:~/www/kz/local-setup$ podman run -d -p 8080:50002 template-local
7681cbdc4a43efce4e0cead6838a475fd6fe11f7d32018d9ed2df2b4fbe2d523
harshit@fedora:~/www/kz/local-setup$
```
But same thing if i do on a different terminal window, then i get different result.
```bash
harshit@fedora:~$ podman run -d -p 8080:50002 template-local
? Please select an image: 
  ▸ docker.io/library/template-local:latest
    ghcr.io/template-local:latest
    quay.io/template-local:latest
```
It asks me to pull the image from a remote hub/repo. which indicates that it cannot access the local image.

comments.body:
The issue was that I've installed VS Code through snap, but what snap does is create a different confinement under the user directory to version its packages and run them.

## How To Debug
Run `podman info | grep graphRoot` inside your vs-code integrated terminal.

```shell
graphRoot: /home/harshit/snap/code/187/.local/share/containers/storage
```
Now run the same command in your terminal outside vs-code.

```shell
graphRoot: /home/harshit/.local/share/containers/storage
```
Observe that inside vs-code the images are read and stored at a different location. Hence it seems they have 2 totally different context.

## Solution

Edit `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` and add the following code
```bash
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "/home/<your-username>/.local/share/containers/storage"
```
replace `<your-username>` with your actual username, and restart vs-code and test with `podman info | grep graphRoot`
